sentence: Subjective:

HPI: The patient, Mr. Nelson, is a 73-year-old male with ongoing management of rheumatoid arthritis, presenting with complaints of persistent swelling in his right knee, which began approximately 2 months ago.
He reports a pain level of 3 out of 10, with a typical pain level of 6 on a normal day.
The swelling has not been relieved by compression socks, and he experiences stiffness and difficulty getting up from a seated position, requiring the use of a cane.
He denies any fever.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of rheumatoid arthritis and has been taking Xeljanz (tofacitinib) 11 mg daily and Celebrex (celecoxib) 200 mg daily for the past 2 months.
Review of systems: The patient reports no additional symptoms in other organs.
Current medications:
Xeljanz (tofacitinib) 11 mg daily for rheumatoid arthritis
Celebrex (celecoxib) 200 mg daily for rheumatoid arthritis
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned
Physical exam: The patient's knee is swollen, with pitting around the knee and ankle.
Range of motion is slightly diminished.
Normal capillary refill in the toes.
The knee is hot, indicating inflammation.
Test Results: Not mentioned
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a flare-up of rheumatoid arthritis in his right knee, with significant swelling and inflammation.
Problem list: Rheumatoid arthritis; active; Rheumatoid arthritis flare-up; active
Plan:
I prescribed a steroid injection of 80 mg of triamcinolone and 1 ml of lidocaine to the patient's right knee.
I also ordered an x-ray of the knee to evaluate the cause of the swelling.
The patient will follow up with his primary care physician to monitor his CMP levels, which are currently within normal limits.
I recommended reducing his salt intake and elevating his feet, especially at night, to help alleviate the swelling.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with his primary care physician to monitor his CMP levels and to discuss further management of his rheumatoid arthritis.
